cards.title: Refining Margins Hit Record Highs Amidst Global Supply Constraints
cards.body: Global refining margins have surged to unprecedented levels, driven by a combination of supply disruptions and robust demand recovery post-pandemic. According to Platts, margins for complex refineries in Europe have exceeded $15 per barrel, the highest in over a decade. This trend is expected to persist as maintenance schedules and geopolitical tensions continue to constrain supply.

cards.title: U.S. Diesel Crack Spreads Narrow as Inventories Replenish
cards.body: Diesel crack spreads in the U.S. have narrowed to $25 per barrel, down from $30 earlier this year, as refineries ramp up production following winter maintenance. Argus reports that diesel inventories have increased by 10% in April, alleviating some of the supply pressures seen in the first quarter. This inventory build-up is expected to stabilize diesel prices in the near term.

cards.title: Asian Jet Fuel Demand Surges, Prices Follow Suit
cards.body: Jet fuel demand in Asia has surged by 12% year-on-year, driven by a strong recovery in air travel and tourism. OGJ Downstream notes that jet fuel prices have risen accordingly, with Singapore jet fuel trading at $120 per barrel, a 20% increase since January. The upward trend is likely to continue as airlines expand capacity to meet rising passenger numbers.

cards.title: Ethylene Margins Contract Amid Rising Feedstock Costs
cards.body: Ethylene margins have narrowed significantly in Q1 2026, primarily due to a 15% increase in naphtha prices since January. The ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have exacerbated supply constraints, impacting the cost structure for major producers like ExxonMobil and Dow Chemical.

cards.title: Polyolefin Demand Surges Driven by Packaging Industry Growth
cards.body: The global demand for polyolefins is projected to grow by 6% in 2026, fueled by increased consumption in the packaging sector. The shift towards sustainable packaging solutions has accelerated investments in bio-based polyolefins, with companies like SABIC and BASF leading the charge.

cards.title: New Cracker Projects Set to Boost Ethylene Output
cards.body: Several new cracker projects are slated for completion in 2026, promising to increase global ethylene capacity by 3 million tonnes per annum. Notable projects include Shell's Pennsylvania Petrochemicals Complex and Formosa Plastics' new facility in Texas, which are expected to come online by Q4.

cards.title: ExxonMobil Sanctions New LNG Train in Mozambique
cards.body: ExxonMobil has sanctioned a new LNG train in Mozambique, expected to add 5 million tonnes per annum (mtpa) to its existing capacity by 2029. This decision, announced on April 18, 2026, reflects the company's commitment to expanding its footprint in the high-demand Asian markets, leveraging Mozambique's strategic location and vast reserves.

cards.title: Shell Achieves First Oil at Bonga North Project
cards.body: Shell has announced the first oil from its Bonga North project offshore Nigeria, marking a significant milestone in the company's deepwater operations. The project, which commenced production on April 15, 2026, is expected to peak at 150,000 barrels per day, enhancing Nigeria's oil output amidst fluctuating global prices.

cards.title: TotalEnergies Awards Offshore Drilling Contract in Brazil
cards.body: TotalEnergies has awarded a $500 million offshore drilling contract to Seadrill for operations in the Campos Basin, Brazil. The contract, announced on April 19, 2026, involves the deployment of two ultra-deepwater rigs, underscoring TotalEnergies' strategic focus on boosting its production in the region's pre-salt reserves.

cards.title: UK's First SMR Project Achieves Key Milestone
cards.body: The UK's first Small Modular Reactor (SMR) project, led by Rolls-Royce, has achieved a significant milestone with the completion of its regulatory assessment phase. This marks a crucial step towards the construction of the first unit, expected to be operational by 2029. The project aims to deliver up to 10 SMRs, contributing significantly to the UK's net-zero targets.

cards.title: China Completes Construction of World's Largest Nuclear Reactor
cards.body: China has completed the construction of the world's largest nuclear reactor, the Hualong One, at the Fuqing plant. The reactor, with a capacity of 1,200 MW, is expected to be operational by the end of 2026. This development underscores China's commitment to expanding its nuclear capacity to meet growing energy demands and reduce carbon emissions.

cards.title: US Grid-Scale Battery Storage Contracts Surge in 2026
cards.body: The US has seen a surge in grid-scale battery storage contracts in the first quarter of 2026, with over 5 GW of new projects announced. This trend is driven by increased demand for renewable integration and grid reliability, with major utilities like NextEra Energy and Duke Energy leading the charge. The rapid deployment of storage solutions is expected to significantly enhance grid resilience and support the transition to renewable energy sources.
